sections.paragraph:
μεταστρέφω fut. μεταστρέψω; 1 aor. μετέστρεψα. Pass.: 2 fut. μεταστραφήσομαι; 2 aor. μετεστράφην; impv. μεταστραφήτω (Hom. et al.; PGM 4, 2625; LXX) to cause a change in state or condition, change, alter τὶ εἴς τι someth. into someth., oft. its opposite (Ps 77:44; Sir 11:31 τὰ ἀγαθὰ εἰς κακά. Cp. TestAsh 1:8) sun into darkness Ac 2:20 (Jo 3:4). Laughter to grief Js 4:9 v.l. (cp. Am 8:10; 1 Macc 9:41; ParJer 6:6). W. acc. of thing μ. τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ Χριστοῦ change or distort the gospel of Christ Gal 1:7.—Of Mary’s influence on the disciples μετέστρεψεν τὸν νοῦν αὐτῶν ἐ̣[πʼ ἀγαθόν] (Mary) changed their mind [for the better] GMary Ox 3525, 13.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

someth. someth. = something

oft. oft. = often

TestAsh TestAsh = Testament of Asher, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

acc. acc. = accusative

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 641
